title: pouteria boninensis
intro: Pouteria boninensis, also known as the Bonin oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Bonin Islands in Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pouteria boninensis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and improve overall cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics that can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
